LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153 --- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANT LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. CROSS-DEFENDANT HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THERE WAS NO CONTRACT BETWEEN HIM AND CROSS-PLAINTIFF DIANA LAU AND THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT NEVER PROMISED TO PAY RENT. IN OPPOSITION, CROSS-PLAINTIFF RELIES SOLELY ON ALLEGED ADMISSIONS MADE IN LEE'S ANSWER TO THE UD COMPLAINT. HOWEVER, LEE

DEFENDANTS SUET YIM DIANA LAU AND YUN-FAI CHRIS LAU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CONSIDERATION FOR THE PROMISSORY NOTE WAS EVER PAID. THE TERM "FOR VALUE RECEIVED" IN THE PROMISSORY NOTE IS AMBIGUOUS AND PAROLE EVIDENCE IS, THEREFORE, ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH ITS MEANING. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT THE "VALUE RECEIVED" WAS IN FACT A CONTEMPORANEOUS ORAL PROMISE BY DEFENDANT DIANA LAU TO PLAINTIFF TO GIVE HER THE $300,000 IS A REASONABLE INTERPRETATION OF THE AMBIGUOUS TERM, THUS PRESENTING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER ON MOTION. COURT RULED ON BOTH PLAINTIFFS AND DEFENDANTS OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, ALL OVERRULED. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 (302)
MINI-MINUTES FOR SEP-01-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-13-2011 Off Calendar

Defendant Suet Lau, Yun-Fai Lau Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-01-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS SUET YIM DIANA LAU AND YUN-FAI CHRIS LAU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Daniel Cano, Esq. for Svetlana M. Shirinova, Esq. 947-0703 for pltf & opposing party; Lawrence M. Scancarelli, Esq. of Law Offices of Lawrence M. Scancarelli 398-1644 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE CONSIDERATION FOR THE PROMISSORY NOTE WAS EVER PAID. THE TERM "FOR VALUE RECEIVED" IN THE PROMISSORY NOTE IS AMBIGUOUS AND PAROLE EVIDENCE IS, THEREFORE, ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH ITS MEANING. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT THE "VALUE RECEIVED" WAS IN FACT A CONTEMPORANEOUS ORAL PROMISE BY DEFENDANT DIANA LAU TO PLAINTIFF TO GIVE HER THE $300,000 IS A REASONABLE INTERPRETATION OF THE AMBIGUOUS TERM, THUS PRESENTING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order on motion. Court ruled on both plaintiffs and defendants objections to evidence, all overruled. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-14-2011 Continued To Jan-03-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS SUET LAU, YUN-FAI LAU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Danniel Cano (947.0703) for plaintiff and responding party; Lawrence M. Scancarelli of the Law Offices of Lawrence M. Scancarelli (398.1644) for defendants and moving parties; Oliver Gutierrez of the Law Offices of Oliver R. Gutierrez (650.363.7295) for intervenor James Chao.
Defendant submitted a courtesy copy of their reply papers and the court continued the matter to September 1, 2011. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Danniel Cano (947.0703) for cross defendant and moving party; Lawrence M. Scancarelli of the Law Offices of Lawrence M. Scancarelli (398.1644) for cross-complainant and responding party; Oliver Gutierrez of the Law Offices of Oliver R. Gutierrez (650.363.7295) for intervenor James Chao.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Cross-Defendant Lee's Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is granted. Cross-Defendant has shifted the burden to show that there was no contract between him and Cross-Plaintiff Diana Lau and that Cross-Defendant never promised to pay rent. In opposition, Cross-Plaintiff relies solely on alleged admissions made in Lee's Answer to the UD Complaint. However, Lee never admitted in his Answer that he was a tenant since 2005 or that Cross-Plaintiff was entitled to rent. To the contrary, Cross-Defendant answered that there was no oral agreement between the parties to pay rent and that Cross-Plaintiff told them they could live in the apartment rent free. Because the Breach of Contract fails, the common count claim based on the alleged breach of Contract also fails. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Cross-defendant to submit a proposed form of order.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
